name: Human Systems
summary: A path for people who want to understand people, institutions, incentives, politics, economics, and the behavior of groups over time.
thesis: Build judgment about how humans coordinate, compete, persuade, and govern.
fitSignals: Cares about politics, economics, relationships, and communication
Wants to understand incentives and power
Likes historical examples and social context
Is interested in how decisions move through institutions

defaults.reviewRhythm: Regular weekly study with one applied reflection session
defaults.milestoneTemplate: Explain a real human-system problem using {coreSubject}, {supportingTopic}, and the {role} lens

weeklyCadencePresets.gentle: 2 reflective sessions and one short review block per week
weeklyCadencePresets.balanced: 3 to 4 sessions per week with one applied case study
weeklyCadencePresets.ambitious: 5 sessions per week with ongoing note synthesis
weeklyCadencePresets.obsessive: Daily reading, synthesis, and decision analysis
